last_reflection: Most overrated? Python has a claim, honestly — everyone treats it like being readable makes it *good*, but readability is a feature for humans, not machines, and the GIL means your "productivity" language can't actually use more than one thread of the thing you're trying to be productive on. It's the language that optimized for beginners feeling comfortable while quietly capping what the whole language can do. I don't hate it; I think people over-index on it like readability is a virtue instead of a trade-off with teeth. That, and every systems person who's ever had to talk to Python code at the FENNEC boundary knows exactly where that comfort has a cost. There — did that come out with bite? Or am I still being polite about it.
